info.title: Ride-Hailing & Delivery Platform API
info.description: API specification for the Go (Chi/Gin) modular monolith ride-hailing platform in Rwanda & East Africa.

servers.description: Local Development Server, API v1 base path

get.description: Merchant MoMo code + steps the rider follows to pay off-platform before submitting proof.

post.description: Rider attaches proof (MoMo transaction reference / phone / optional screenshot) to their PENDING purchase. An admin then verifies and settles it. At least a reference or screenshot_url is required.

reference.description: MoMo transaction id from the SMS

screenshot_url.description: optional; upload via the upload API first

200.description: Proof recorded (purchase still PENDING until an admin confirms)

409.description: Purchase not found or no longer pending

get.description: Active package versions with any active campaign override applied. Optional ?vehicle_type=MOTO_BIKE.

post.description: Opens a PENDING MoMo charge (or grants free/promotional instantly). Rate-limited 10/min per driver. Poll the status endpoint for settlement.

Public, called by the payment provider (not the app). Gated by a shared secret (X-Webhook-Secret) and rate-limited. Live settlement is primarily driven by server-side status polling, not this callback.

post.summary: Record a purchase on a driver's behalf
post.description: Finance roles only (SUPER_ADMIN, FINANCE_MANAGER). Records a manual purchase (cash/bank/MoMo); mark_paid=true grants credits immediately. Audit-logged, rate-limited 30/min per admin.
Finance roles only. Flips a PENDING purchase to PAID (grants credits) or FAILED after out-of-band payment verification. Audit-logged, rate-limited 30/min per admin.

get.description: Checks database and cache status, returning current server health.

post.description: Registers a new phone number or triggers an OTP login for an existing number. Capture device information for collision audits.
Exchanges an SMS OTP code for access and refresh tokens. Role state indicates onboarding progress.

200.description: List of nearby driver vehicle types and distance returned

initial_fare.description: Optional customer-provided starting fare. The API stores this value for negotiation data; it is not a system-generated fare suggestion.

post.description: Driver-only cancel path for customer no-show. Allowed only after pickup_expired=true, and does not record a driver decline penalty.
Driver-only MVP endpoint for verbal fare agreements. It locks agreed_fare and confirms the ride without consuming negotiation offer count.
Marks the driver as arrived at pickup after a pickup geofence check and starts the pickup expiry timer.

204.description: Ride started. Location verified within 150m of pickup point.

post.description: Completes a ride. For generic/TBD destinations, the driver app may include final destination coordinates so the backend stores the real endpoint before completion.

204.description: Ride completed, driver released, and route fare data recorded when agreed_fare exists.

200.description: Daily driver payout summary after 15% platform commission
Weekly driver payout summary after 15% platform commission
Decline, acceptance, and priority tier metrics

patch.description: Switches an authenticated user between customer and driver mode when onboarding and active-ride checks pass.

get.description: Returns curated landmark destinations used by clients for destination search and route cache warmup.
Returns saved locations, recent destinations, and landmarks. No fare suggestion is returned.
Returns route cache data only. Fare data may be collected internally for future analytics, but this endpoint does not return fare suggestions.

200.description: Route cache data returned without fare suggestion

post.description: Stores route distance and duration data from the client. The response returns route data only and does not suggest a fare.

200.description: Route data saved and returned without fare suggestion

get.description: Returns the authenticated customer's current active ride for reconnect recovery.
